labels.ragCaption: Dense + Sparse + Graph hybrid retrieval architecture

profile.status: Study focus and finding future job.
profile.headline: CS/AI Master's Degree in AI Research · Engineer Perspective
profile.summaryLead: A software engineer who builds efficient and scalable systems with a passion for the intersection of algorithms and machine learning inspired by nature.
summary: From Python · C++ · CUDA-based AI experiments to web front-end, if you are interested in technology, you are a research developer who creates and experiments directly. All projects should be developed based on planning focus as they should be on solid roots. They also aim at sustainable lightweight architectures without unnecessary complexity, and should be very friendly to and followed by agent coding such as Codex and Claude Code.
Currently in the CS/AI master’s program, we focus on research/development aimed at research and engineering careers in the fields of LLM systems and RAG architecture.

Since graduation, the majority of projects related to deployment have been interested in deployment, environmental matching and automation, and they have built experimental environments that can be replicated with DevContainer and Docker.
As a hobby, we are experimenting with various domains such as voice/voice-related model learning, Edge Environment LLM reasoning, and real-time voice conversion, and we are conducting a top project.

education.note: Research focus: LLM systems, RAG architecture, and lightweight inference

agentic-workflow.title: Agentic Workflow & MCP
agentic-workflow.desc: Designing agent workflows based on the Model Context Protocol, including a cross-review automation system with a human approval loop.
agentic-workflow.body:
## Interest

I design workflows where AI agents can use tools safely while operating with human approval loops.

audio-voice.title: Music Source Separation and Voice Conversion
audio-voice.desc: Training experiments with MDX23C and Demucs4HT architectures, RVC / Applio-based voice conversion, and Hydra-configured experiment management.
audio-voice.body:
## Interest

I work with music source separation and voice conversion models in reproducible experiment environments.

edge-llm.title: Lightweight LLM Inference and Edge AI
edge-llm.desc: Building local inference environments with llama.cpp, Qwen3, and GGUF quantization while exploring practical LLM deployment on constrained hardware.
edge-llm.body:
## Interest

I explore practical LLM inference setups that can run on constrained hardware.

rag.title: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)
rag.desc: Hybrid retrieval with Dense / Sparse / Graph channels, Weighted RRF integration, and domain-specific document indexing, with hands-on experience building RAG pipelines for aerospace documents.
rag.body:
## Interest

I experiment with hybrid RAG architectures that combine Dense, Sparse, and Graph retrieval and integrate them through Weighted RRF.

## Current Focus

- Domain-specific document chunking
- Retrieval quality evaluation
- Source-grounded answer generation

aerospace-rag.name: aerospace-rag
aerospace-rag.desc: A RAG system for aerospace work documents. It combines Qdrant + BM25 + Graph three-channel retrieval, Weighted RRF fusion, and Ollama-based answer generation, optimized for a Colab T4 environment.
aerospace-rag.metric: Improved MRR by 31% over single-channel retrieval through three-channel hybrid search
aerospace-rag.body:
## Overview

A RAG system that combines Dense, Sparse, and Graph retrieval channels for aerospace work documents.

cross-review-bridge.name: cross-review-bridge
cross-review-bridge.desc: A human-approved cross-review workflow with external AI reviewers, automated as a Codex skill.
cross-review-bridge.metric: Reduced the code review cycle by about 70% compared with manual review
cross-review-bridge.body:
## Overview

An automation system that connects external AI reviewers and Codex through a human-approved review loop.

introduce-cv-page.name: IntroduceCvPage (namu-log)
introduce-cv-page.desc: A blog + CV system where writing in a WYSIWYG Markdown editor automatically creates GitHub PRs. Built with Next.js 16 and Tiptap.
introduce-cv-page.metric: Fully automated the flow from article publishing to PR creation
introduce-cv-page.body:
## Overview

A blog and CV system that experiments with a GitHub PR-based publishing workflow.

llm-rag-research.name: LLM + RAG Research System
llm-rag-research.desc: A private repository for comparing RAG strategies, building retrieval quality evaluation pipelines, and researching domain-adaptive chunking strategies.
llm-rag-research.metric: Compared five RAG strategies and observed a 23% gap in Recall@5
llm-rag-research.body:
## Overview

A private research system for comparing and evaluating different RAG strategies.

mediamtx-installer.name: mediamtx-installer
mediamtx-installer.desc: An automation script that installs a MediaMTX streaming server on Ubuntu 24.04 with a single command.
mediamtx-installer.metric: Reduced setup time from 15 minutes manually to about 30 seconds
mediamtx-installer.body:
## Overview

An operations automation script that simplifies the MediaMTX installation process.

music-source-separation.name: Music-Source-Separation
music-source-separation.desc: Training music source separation models based on MDX23C and Demucs4HT architectures, with Hydra configs for experiment management and CUDA-based GPU training.
music-source-separation.metric: Reached 8.2 dB vocal separation SDR with a Demucs4HT-based setup
music-source-separation.body:
## Overview

A project for training music source separation models and managing experiment configuration.

spring-community-board.name: SpringCommunityBoard
spring-community-board.desc: A CRUD community board built with Spring Boot and JPA, including member management, posts, and comments.
spring-community-board.metric: Improved list query response time by 40% by resolving a JPA N+1 issue
spring-community-board.body:
## Overview

A Spring Boot-based CRUD board with member, post, and comment features.

rag-evaluation.title: RAG Evaluation Notes
rag-evaluation.summary: A practical evaluation lens for comparing RAG systems across retrieval quality, answer grounding, and operating cost.
rag-evaluation.body:
## Why Evaluation Comes First

If you only look at answer quality in a RAG system, it is easy to miss the cause of failure. The retrieval, reranking, and generation stages need to be separated so you can see whether documents were missed, pushed down, or ignored during answer generation.

## Basic Metrics

- Recall@5: checks whether the supporting evidence appears in the top five retrieved documents.
- MRR: measures how early the first useful evidence appears.
- Groundedness: checks whether the answer is actually supported by retrieved documents.
